pattern: Every cell's key is its Manhattan distance from the center, so producing the cells in any order that never decreases that distance is enough — a simple sort by distance does it.
hints: The required order only constrains distance to be non-decreasing; cells at equal distance may appear in any relative order.
Compute the Manhattan distance of each cell to the center and use it as a sort key.
Generate all cell coordinates, sort them by their distance to the center, and return them; ties need no special handling.

edge_inputs.description: rectangle with center at an edge
center in the middle so ties radiate symmetrically

stress.description: random small grid with a random center
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 12)))
    cols = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 12)))
    rc = rng.randint(0, rows - 1)
    cc = rng.randint(0, cols - 1)
    return [rows, cols, rc, cc]
stress.note: kept small; output is the full grid of cells
